content:
# Final Review Planner Positions
**Producer:** agent:claude/claude-opus-4-6

## Summary

All four amendments identify real correctness or robustness issues in the task-run lineage tracking code. The debug_assert gap (fr-10-681307ea) is a clear safety defect. The plan_hash race on new entries (fr-10-fa9c76f6) is a real consistency issue worth documenting or fixing. The permanent orphaning of legacy plan_hash=None rows (fr-10-9757053d) is a valid gap in the repair path. The overly broad uniqueness check (fr-10-a0a290d3) is a correctness bug that prevents valid backfill scenarios.

## Votes

- `fr-10-681307ea`: Accept
  The debug_assert! at line ~2824 enforces a critical safety invariant: when requested_run_id is Some, requested_started_at must also be Some to prevent backfilling the wrong legacy row. In release builds, debug_assert! is a no-op, so the guard vanishes entirely. While current callers (line 3093-3098) happen to pass (Some(run_id), Some(started_at)) correctly, the contract is documented as essential to correctness, and relying on all future callers to comply without runtime enforcement is fragile. Replacing with an early-return `if` check (returning None) is trivial, safe, and closes a real gap between the documented contract and its enforcement.
- `fr-10-fa9c76f6`: Accept
  The code carefully documents why snapshot-based plan_hash backfill is skipped for existing rows (persist_plan() can advance the snapshot hash after dispatch). But the exact same race applies to new entries: if persist_plan() runs between bead dispatch and record_task_run_start, the new entry gets stamped with the post-evolution snapshot hash rather than the plan version that actually dispatched the bead. The amendment only asks for documenting this known limitation or having callers supply plan_hash explicitly — both are reasonable, low-risk improvements that close a correctness gap in the plan_hash provenance story.
- `fr-10-9757053d`: Accept
  Existing rows with plan_hash=None are permanently orphaned — the code intentionally skips snapshot backfill for existing rows (documented at lines 2988-2992 and 3315-3319) because the snapshot may have advanced. This is a defensible decision for the general case, but it means legacy or partial rows created before plan_hash tracking was introduced can never be labeled, even when the plan has NOT evolved and the snapshot hash is provably correct. The amendment asks for either a safe backfill path (e.g., backfill only when the plan hash hasn't changed since the row was created) or persisting enough provenance to make that determination later. Both are reasonable improvements to close this gap in the repair/replay paths.
- `fr-10-a0a290d3`: Accept
  The uniqueness check in unique_terminal_replay_match (lines 2859-2862) requires that every other row in matching_indices is a legacy start pair for the candidate. But matching_indices contains ALL rows for the same bead/project, including completed attempts from entirely separate runs. So once a bead has been attempted more than once (attempt A terminal + attempt B completed), the check fails for attempt A because attempt B isn't a legacy start pair for A — even though started_at uniquely identifies A. This is a real correctness bug that prevents valid backfill of runless terminal rows in multi-attempt scenarios. Pre-filtering matching_indices by started_at or excluding rows from unrelated attempts before the uniqueness check would fix this without weakening the safety guarantees.
